title: Directory watcher symlink attack: process_file follows symlinks without validation
description:
src/watch.rs watch_loop() receives filesystem events for new files in the watch directory and calls process_file() which calls tokio::fs::read_to_string(file_path) without checking whether the path is a symlink.

Attack path:
1. Attacker has write access to the watch directory (the scenario where this matters)
2. Attacker creates a symlink: ln -s /etc/passwd watched_dir/post.md
3. The notify watcher fires a Create event for the symlink
4. After the 200ms sleep, process_file reads the symlink target (/etc/passwd)
5. The content is passed to markdown_to_html() -> sanitize_html() and stored as a post, then delivered to all followers via ActivityPub

The content of /etc/passwd is sanitized before delivery, but the file has been read and the post is published. More dangerous targets: private keys in data_dir, config files with webhook keys, SQLite DB journal files.

Mitigation: check file_path.symlink_metadata() and reject if the file_type is a symlink before reading.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
